MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Richard Canatella for and with Lee Arthur McClothin (Petitioner). Greer Smith for and with Willie Ann Davis (Executor). Martha Slaughter in Propria Persona.

The Court stated the tentative as follows:
1st cause of action to revoke probate of will and 4th cause of action for elder abuse. Tentative ruling: Sustained with 10 days for leave to amend to state facts showing that Lee Arthur McGlothin is an interested person if decedent Berry's will that is before the Court for probate is revoked.

2nd cause of action to cancel deed and 3rd cause of action to quiet title. Tentative ruling: Overruled. There are no points and authorities in support of these cause of action.

The Parties presented oral argument to support their positions. After hearing argument, the Court adopted the tentative ruling. Ms. Greer and Ms. Slaughter are to prepare and serve the orders on Mr. Canatella.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Karen Farnsworth, CSR#5162. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Garrido.

Verified Petition To Revoke Probate Of Last Will, To Cancel Deed Conveying Real Property Belonging To Petitioner And Quiet Title To Real Property Purportedly Property Of The Estate But Claimed By Petitioner And For Damages For Elder Abuse
